page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. This x86_64 architecture server features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading, providing two threads per core. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance includes 2458 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing it in the top 25% of tested servers, while multi-core CPU performance falls in the average tier. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with a strong PassMark memory score, though raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks register in the average range. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong image processing capabilities and strong text generation speeds for small language models. However, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in large-scale AI workloads, showing weak prompt processing speeds for large 70B language models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ads_v5 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, balancing a massive 512.0 GB RAM allocation with a moderate 32 vCPU count. This configuration reduces compute overheads for applications that require large memory footprints but do not scale linearly with high core counts. The inclusion of 2458 GB of local storage further enhances its value for data-intensive operations. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine configured with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage. The server features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and a strong PassMark memory score, though multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. It shows strong text generation for small language models but exhibits weak prompt processing for large 70B models. This memory-optimized configuration, which lacks a GPU, is designed for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM and local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio, dedicated AMD EPYC processors, and substantial local storage for data-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
512.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
